post.content:
These are not exact questions. 4questions(60mins)
Q1. Find the minimum number of moves to make array contiguous. A move is changing an array value to some other. For e.g. [4,5,1,8,10]
Ans is 2. Change 1->7 and 10->6, the array becomes [4,5,7,8,6].

Q2. Given an array of integers representing rods. Given k, find the maximum number l s.t. we can construct atlease k rods of length l.
E.x. [2,9,5,4,8] and k=4. Ans=4.

Q3. Grid of size m,n with values 0 and 1 representing free and occupied cell. Initially robot is at 0,0 and battery level say k, you need to visit maximum cells with value 0 and return to 0,0 before battery drains out. Each move causes battery to drain by 1.
For e.g. 
000
010
000 and battery=6. 
Ans=3(Beacause you have to return to 0,0 and battery>0).

Q4. Given an array. You need to do these three operation.
Remove any p elements.
Remove any q pair of elements. (2 consequtive elements)
Remove any r triplet of elements.
Return the max sum of the array after all the elements are removed?
They are asking LC hard questions in the OA round.\nQ1 is this https://leetcode.com/problems/minimum-number-of-operations-to-make-array-continuous/description/
